80
18
27 

Rulings. F-01–05, F-09–11, F-13–19, F-21–24 are recorded as stated and will be reflected verbatim in the next doc revision. Four need a word back from you — I've picked defaults so a silent "fine" is a valid answer:

F-06: Cloudflare tunnels it is, with one sharp edge I verified: trycloudflare.com is on the Public Suffix List, so on quick tunnels a passkey binds to the random per-run subdomain and dies with it. Durable passkeys therefore require a named tunnel on your stable domain; quick tunnels stay fine for demos with password+TOTP. I'll also carry your "ROBUST Argon2" instruction into a custom IPasswordHasher (Argon2id, ~64 MiB / t=3 / p=1, configurable), and note the accepted risk that in-house ordering hairpins through Cloudflare, so LAN ordering depends on WAN health. I'm taking GoTunnels as the tunnel/TLS/observability reference only — its never-expiring localStorage bearer session doesn't fit Blazor Server's cookie/circuit model.
F-07 (Q1): I'll respec as one living order per guest per sitting: edits staged client-side and sent explicitly, each send is one batch event and one kitchen alert (not per-keystroke, to avoid alert fatigue); lines get a pending → fulfilled lifecycle replacing order-level Acknowledged; guests may remove only not-yet-fulfilled lines. Veto if you want literal per-change notifications.
F-08 (Q2/Q3): Default: TOTP is challenged on password sign-ins only, not passkey sign-ins (the authenticator is already a second factor). Admin reset wipes password+TOTP; next sign-in forces new password, then immediate TOTP re-enrollment before anything else. The per-user "admin TOTP toggle" from §4.2 disappears.
F-12 (Q4/Q5): New role table_display with surface /display/{table} — a cheap per-table device paired once via an admin one-time code, showing a QR that encodes {origin}/table/{id}?token=HMAC(table_secret, time_window), rotating every 60 s, current+previous window accepted, rotation independent of sitting state; counter can read the current code off their own screen if a display dies. Q5: I'm speccing rotating-only — printed static QRs are gone entirely rather than kept as a flagged fallback. Veto if you want the fallback documented.

all of this is fine. 
Please generate the full code base as well as all the documentation
I have uploaded the latest dump.txt 
git repository is/will be publicly available at 
https://github.com/kusl/myrestaurant


/
Rulings. F-01–05, F-09–11, F-13–19, F-21–24 are recorded as stated and will be reflected verbatim in the next doc revision. Four need a word back from you — I've picked defaults so a silent "fine" is a valid answer:
F-06: Cloudflare tunnels it is, with one sharp edge I verified: trycloudflare.com is on the Public Suffix List, so on quick tunnels a passkey binds to the random per-run subdomain and dies with it. Durable passkeys therefore require a named tunnel on your stable domain; quick tunnels stay fine for demos with password+TOTP. I'll also carry your "ROBUST Argon2" instruction into a custom IPasswordHasher (Argon2id, ~64 MiB / t=3 / p=1, configurable), and note the accepted risk that in-house ordering hairpins through Cloudflare, so LAN ordering depends on WAN health. I'm taking GoTunnels as the tunnel/TLS/observability reference only — its never-expiring localStorage bearer session doesn't fit Blazor Server's cookie/circuit model.
F-07 (Q1): I'll respec as one living order per guest per sitting: edits staged client-side and sent explicitly, each send is one batch event and one kitchen alert (not per-keystroke, to avoid alert fatigue); lines get a pending → fulfilled lifecycle replacing order-level Acknowledged; guests may remove only not-yet-fulfilled lines. Veto if you want literal per-change notifications.
F-08 (Q2/Q3): Default: TOTP is challenged on password sign-ins only, not passkey sign-ins (the authenticator is already a second factor). Admin reset wipes password+TOTP; next sign-in forces new password, then immediate TOTP re-enrollment before anything else. The per-user "admin TOTP toggle" from §4.2 disappears.
F-12 (Q4/Q5): New role table_display with surface /display/{table} — a cheap per-table device paired once via an admin one-time code, showing a QR that encodes {origin}/table/{id}?token=HMAC(table_secret, time_window), rotating every 60 s, current+previous window accepted, rotation independent of sitting state; counter can read the current code off their own screen if a display dies. Q5: I'm speccing rotating-only — printed static QRs are gone entirely rather than kept as a flagged fallback. Veto if you want the fallback documented.
all of this is fine. 
Please generate the full code base as well as all the documentation

https://github.com/kusl/myrestaurant
